what-is-an-api.instruction: Explain what an API is to a non-technical coworker. Use a real-world analogy.
what-is-an-api.hint: An API is like a drive-through window. You pull up, read the menu (documentation), place your order (request), and get your food (response) without stepping inside.
what-is-an-api.goodExample: An API is like a drive-through window. You pull up, read the menu (documentation), place your order into the speaker (send a request), and get your food (response) without ever stepping inside the kitchen.
what-is-an-api.successMessage: Great analogy! Making APIs tangible is a superpower for cross-team communication.

triggers-and-actions.instruction: Design a trigger-action automation for a real business scenario. Specify the trigger event, conditions/filters, and resulting action(s).
triggers-and-actions.hint: When a new lead fills out the contact form (trigger), if company size is 50+ (filter), then: add to CRM as Enterprise Lead, send welcome email, notify sales in Slack.
triggers-and-actions.successMessage: Solid automation design! The condition/filter step separates noisy automations from useful ones.

your-first-automation.instruction: Describe step-by-step how you'd build an automation that saves email attachments to cloud storage and notifies your team. Include tools.
your-first-automation.hint: Step 1: Gmail trigger in Make.com. Step 2: Download attachment. Step 3: Upload to Google Drive. Step 4: Slack notification with file link.
your-first-automation.successMessage: You just designed a real automation! This pattern saves teams hours every week.

smart-routing.instruction: Design a smart routing system for incoming customer support tickets. Define at least 3 routes based on urgency, topic, or customer tier.
smart-routing.successMessage: Smart routing eliminates the manual triage bottleneck.

build-ai-workflow.instruction: Design an AI-powered workflow that takes raw customer feedback and produces an actionable insights report. Include each step.
build-ai-workflow.successMessage: This is a production-grade AI workflow. You're thinking like an automation architect.

api-playground.instruction: Write a prompt asking Claude to generate a curl command that calls a weather API for your city. Include the endpoint structure.
api-playground.successMessage: APIs stop being scary once you've written your first curl command.

workflow-quiz.instruction: A client's automation breaks every Monday morning. Emails stop forwarding to their CRM. List 3 possible causes and how you'd investigate each.
workflow-quiz.successMessage: Systematic debugging is the difference between an automation user and an automation architect.

automation-quiz.instruction: You're building an automation suite for a 20-person marketing team. They need social scheduling, lead capture, email sequences, and reporting. Outline the architecture.
automation-quiz.successMessage: You're thinking at the systems level now. This is the architect mindset.

api-quiz.instruction: Write a prompt asking Claude to explain REST vs webhook-based integrations, with a practical example of when to use each.
api-quiz.successMessage: Understanding REST vs webhooks is fundamental to designing efficient automations.
